title: Get a DP-10 contributions certificate
description: A summary of the National Insurance contributions paid for an employee during a tax year.
summary: The DP-10 is a National Insurance Contributions Certificate. Employers issue one to every employee at the end of each tax year. It shows the contributions that have been paid for the employee during that year.
You use the DP-10 mainly for your annual tax return – it is the document the Barbados Revenue Authority looks at to confirm your NIS contributions for the year.
If you have lost your DP-10, your first step is to ask your employer for a replacement. They are required to issue one for each tax year you worked there.
If you cannot get it from your employer – for example, because the company has closed – the National Insurance Office can usually issue a replacement directly.

items: the employer’s registration number and business name
the tax year the certificate covers

how_long: Replacements are usually ready within 10 working days.

options.heading: Through your employer
content: Ask your employer for a DP-10. Employers must issue one for each employee every tax year.

options.heading: Directly from the NIS
content: If you cannot get a copy from your employer, ask the National Insurance Office for a replacement.

assumptions: ‘Replacements are usually ready within 10 working days’ is an estimate.
